cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the statements (A to D) is true according to the passage. I need to go through each option and check against the information given in the text. First, let's recall the key points from the passage. The main topic is about the British government reviewing the use of lotteries to allocate school places because more than 20% of children are not getting into their first-choice schools in some areas. The passage mentions that in parts of the country, over 20% can't get into their first choice. For example, in Richmond-upon-Thames, 62% got their first choice, down from 64% the previous year, meaning 38% didn't. In Tower Hamlet, 71.1% got their first choice, so 28.9% didn't.
